data.title: Cattle Higher Ahead of Cattle on Feed Report
data.description:
Live cattle futures are trading with stronger midday action in Friday, up 50 to 75 cents in the nearbys. Cash trade has been slow this week, with Thursday sales at $199 in the South and North trade at $199.50-200. Feeder cattle are posting $1.50 to $2 higher trade so far at midday. The CME Feeder Cattle Index was up another 30 cents on February 19 at $278.81. 

Beef export sales totaled 21,481 MT in the week of February 13, up 64% from the week prior. Japan purchased a total of 9,800 MT, with 3,400 MT sold to South Korea. Export Shipments were 14,193 MT, a 4-week low. The largest destination was Japan at 4,100 MT, with 3,800 MT to South Korea. 

Friday’s afternoon’s cattle on Feed report is expected to show a drop of 0.8% from the year prior as of February 1. Placements in January are expected to be up 2.2%, with marketings up 2.1%.

USDA’s National Wholesale Boxed Beef report was mixed on Friday morning, as the Chc/Sel spread was tightening at $7.79. Choice boxes were down another 76 cents at $311.87/cwt, with Select up 90 cents to $304.08. The Thursday Federally inspected cattle slaughter was estimated at 117,000 head, taking the week to date total to 447,000 head. That is down 7,000 head from the previous week and 13,012 head below the same week last year.

Feb 25 Live Cattle  are at $198.300, up $0.675,

Apr 25 Live Cattle  are at $194.475, up $0.675,

Jun 25 Live Cattle  are at $190.725, up $0.750,

Mar 25 Feeder Cattle  are at $268.500, up $1.675

Apr 25 Feeder Cattle  are at $268.225, up $1.850

May 25 Feeder Cattle  are at $266.825, up $1.750

On the date of publication, Austin Schroeder did not have (either directly or indirectly) positions in any of the securities mentioned in this article. All information and data in this article is solely for informational purposes. For more information please view the Barchart Disclosure Policyhere.

data.title: Warren Buffett's 2025 letter to Berkshire shareholders is coming tomorrow. Watch this number.
data.description:
By Mark Hulbert

Even Buffett is struggling to outperform the stock market. Berkshire's massive cash stake is key.

There's a good chance that Berkshire Hathaway's stock will lag the S&P 500 in the coming years.

This is a matter of simple probabilities, and not a criticism of Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.B) (BRK.A) Chairman and Chief Executive Warren Buffett or his designated successors. Given the huge amount of statistical noise in the market, luck plays a considerable role in performance, not only over the short term but in the intermediate term as well. And since bad luck can happen just as readily as good luck, lagging the market can't be ruled out - even for someone with as phenomenal a track record as Buffett.

Reviewing Buffett's track record in light of these probabilities is a helpful prelude to the anticipated release of Buffett's annual letter to Berkshire shareholders on Saturday.

For 2024, Berkshire Hathaway's stock beat the market, though barely: Its total return was 25.5% for the calendar year, versus 25.0% for the S&P 500 SPX. As you can see from the accompanying chart, the stock is slightly behind the S&P 500 for trailing 10- and 15-year performance, but slightly ahead over the trailing 20 and 30 years. Over each of these periods, the difference between the company's performance and the stock market is small.

To understand why the probabilities don't give much confidence in Berkshire Hathaway's future market-beating abilities, consider how many years it would take to convince a rigorous statistician that the company's past performance was due to genuine ability rather than luck. For illustration purposes, I'll extrapolate into the future the company's performance over the past 30 years - the second half of its 60-year period of being managed by Buffett.

In the first three decades of Buffett's management, the company was much smaller and able to invest in undervalued small-cap companies. Today, Berkshire Hathaway is so huge that, as Buffett has said in past annual meetings, it is only interested in investing in large companies, since only large firms can make a meaningful impact on Berkshire Hathaway's bottom line.

Given the company's annualized margin ahead of the market (alpha) over the past 30 years (1.5%) and the standard deviation of the company's annual returns over this period (19.0%), it would take more than 100 years of such performance to conclude at the 95% confidence level that the company's alpha wasn't attributable to sheer luck.

In other words, there is no practical way for any of us to have statistical confidence that Berkshire Hathaway's market-beating performance over the last 30 years was due to more than luck. That's another way of saying that the best guess is that the company's future performance will closely match that of the overall market.

(Note that a statistician would have reached the opposite conclusion after the first 30 years of Buffett's management of Berkshire Hathaway. Its alpha then was far larger, and as a result those years were more than enough to conclude at the 95% confidence level that the company's performance was due to more than luck. But, as mentioned above, those early years are of limited relevance to forecasting Berkshire Hathaway's future performance.)

Cash on the line

This statistical review of Berkshire Hathaway's performance provides the necessary context to consider any of the topics that Buffett may discuss in the upcoming annual report. We may want to put his opinions in a different light if our best guess is that the company's future performance will closely match that of the market.

This is particularly the case for what will no doubt be one of the most closely watched data points in the annual report: the size of Berkshire Hathaway's cash position, which at the end of 2024's third quarter already stood at a huge $325 billion. Will the company's cash position be even greater at the end of the year, and does that mean Buffett is bearish on the stock market - and how much should we care in any case?

Let me repeat that this analysis is not intended as a criticism of Buffett's phenomenal track record. For virtually every other manager, a statistician's conclusion would be even more sobering. Buffett has defied the law of gravity far longer than virtually any of them. But beating the market becomes harder and harder as Berkshire Hathaway grows larger and larger, and eventually even it succumbs to that law.

Mark Hulbert is a regular contributor to MarketWatch. His Hulbert Ratings tracks investment newsletters that pay a flat fee to be audited. He can be reached at mark@hulbertratings.com.

More: Berkshire buys more Occidental stock. How much it holds now.

Plus: Warren Buffett bought booze, pizza and pool stocks, ditched Ulta Beauty in Q4

-Mark Hulbert

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Exchange-Traded Funds, US Equities Lower After Midday Friday
data.description:
Broad Market Indicators

Broad-market exchange-traded funds IWM and IVV were lower. Actively traded Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) was down 1.1%.

US equity indexes fell in midday trading Friday after a consumer sentiment gauge was revised lower while private sector output growth unexpectedly slowed.

Energy

iShares US Energy ETF (IYE) lost 0.8%, and Energy Select Sector SPDR (XLE) fell 0.7%.

Technology

Technology Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLK) was 1.3% down; iShares US Technology ETF (IYW) and iShares Expanded Tech Sector ETF (IGM) were lower.

SPDR S&P Semiconductor (XSD) lost 1.7%, and iShares Semiconductor (SOXX) was down 2.2%.

Financial

Financial Select Sector SPDR (XLF) fell 0.7%. Direxion Daily Financial Bull 3X Shares (FAS) fell 2.1%, and its bearish counterpart Direxion Daily Financial Bear 3X Shares (FAZ) rose 2.4%.

Commodities

Crude oil declined 2.1%, and the United States Oil Fund (USO) dipped 2.1%. Natural gas rose 4.4%, and the United States Natural Gas Fund (UNG) added 2.3%.

Gold was down 0.1% on Comex, and SPDR Gold Shares (GLD) edged up 0.1%. Silver lost 1.3%, and iShares Silver Trust (SLV) fell 0.6%.

Consumer

Consumer Staples Select Sector SPDR (XLP) rose 0.6%; Vanguard Consumer Staples ETF (VDC) and iShares Dow Jones US Consumer Goods (IYK) were higher.

Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR (XLY) was down 1.9%; retail fund VanEck Vectors Retail ETF (RTH) lost 1.2%, and SPDR S&P Retail (XRT) dropped 2.9%.

Health Care

Health Care Select Sector SPDR (XLV) eased 0.4%, and iShares US Healthcare (IYH) and Vanguard Health Care ETF (VHT) were lower. IShares Biotechnology ETF (IBB) fell 0.1%.

Industrial

Select Sector SPDR-Industrial (XLI) was down 1.6%, and Vanguard Industrials (VIS) and iShares US Industrials (IYJ) were in the red.

data.title: BJ's Restaurants Shares Rise After 4Q Adjusted EPS, Revenue Beats
data.description:
By Chris Wack

Shares of BJ's Restaurants gained after the company reported fourth-quarter adjusted earnings and revenue that beat analysts' expectations.

The restaurant chain's stock was 7.8% higher, at $38.52, Friday afternoon and has been up about 9.1% since the beginning of the year.

BJ's Restaurants said Thursday its quarterly adjusted earnings came in at 47 cents a share, while analysts polled by FactSet were looking for adjusted earnings of 34 cents a share.

Revenue for the quarter was $344.3 million, ahead of the $323.6 million in the same quarter last year and the $337.3 million analysts were expecting.

Comparable restaurant sales were 5.5% higher, with restaurant-level operating profit margin of 15.4%, an increase of 100 basis points, driven primarily by increases in guest traffic, which was positive across all day parts.

For 2025, the Huntington Beach, Calif., company is expecting comparable restaurant sales growth of 2% to 3%, and restaurant-level operating profit of $205 million to $215 million.

Write to Chris Wack at chris.wack@wsj.com

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Friday at 1 PM ET
data.description:
Home Sales Fell 4.9% in January, Extending Slump in Housing Market

Home prices and mortgage rates, which were a drag on home sales for most of 2024, stayed elevated and put off many buyers.

U.S. Consumer Confidence Falls Back on Fears of Tariff-Induced Price Increases

Inflation expectations jumped to 4.3% this month from 3.3% in January, the highest reading since 2023.

UnitedHealth Group Drop Pulls Dow Industrials Lower

The Dow fell moderately on probe of UnitedHealth by the Justice Department, while the S&P 500 and Nasdaq-100 posted smaller declines. Sales of existing homes fell 4.9%.

Europe's Economic Stagnation Persists, Latest Business Surveys Say

The eurozone economy continued to flatline in February, torn between signs of revival in Germany and sharp decline in France.

Chinese Stocks Rally on Strong Alibaba Earnings, DeepSeek Optimism

Upbeat earnings by big tech companies have added to a recent rally driven by Xi Jinping's meeting with top corporate executives and the AI hype around DeepSeek.

In the Trump Era, Flying Green Doesn't Matter Anymore

The president's endorsement has pushed up air-taxi stocks, but development of all types of zero-emission aircraft is falling by the wayside.

U.K. Retail Sales Rise for First Time in Five Months

Retailers in the U.K. reported a brighter start to the new year, with sales rising after four straight months of falls, largely from a recovery in the food segment.

French Industry Sentiment Improves Despite Tariff Threat

The mood in France's factories brightened a little this month as borrowing costs come down, and despite the threat of tariffs on exports to the U.S.

Musk Looks at Auditing the Federal Reserve

The billionaire gives remarks at the Conservative Political Action Conference.

Why Is Warren Buffett Hoarding So Much Cash?

Investors are poised to study the Berkshire Hathaway chairman's annual letter for insights about the stock market.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Friday at 1 PM ET
data.description:
DOJ Investigates Medicare Billing Practices at UnitedHealth

The Justice Department is probing diagnoses and practices that triggered extra payments to UnitedHealth's Medicare Advantage plans, people familiar with the matter say.

FDA Declares Shortage of Novo Nordisk's Wegovy, Ozempic Over

The FDA announcement confirms that the U.S. supply of both prescription-only drugs now meets or exceeds the current and projected demand in the country, the Danish pharmaceutical company said.

Rivian keeps promise on making a profit, but here's why the stock is falling

Rivian Automotive Inc. makes good on its promise to get to its first quarterly gross profit and blows past Wall Street estimates for its sales.

Coinbase Says SEC Intends to Drop Lawsuit Against Crypto Exchange

The agency had sought to oversee the company as a stock market.

Moody's Cuts Nissan Motor's Rating to Junk Status

Moody's said the rating action reflects the Japanese automaker's weak profitability driven by slowing demand for its aging model portfolio.

Trump Media Approves Fund for Mergers, Acquisitions, Partnerships

Trump Media & Technology Group looks to expand tech footprint as it branches out into finance and other industries.

Trump Is Planning to Take Control of the Postal Service, Officials Say

The president is seeking to disband the mail provider's governing board and put the USPS under the control of the Commerce Department.

Thyssenkrupp Says Potential Buyer of HKM Joint Venture Broke Off Talks

The industrial group said discussions with CE Capital Partners about buying Huettenwerke Krupp Mannesmann had collapsed.

The Toughest Job in Fashion? Design Head for a Luxury Brand.

High-end brands are churning through creative directors as they try to revive sales.

U.K. Fines Banks Over Information Sharing on Government Bonds

The competition regulator fined Citi, HSBC, Morgan Stanley and Royal Bank of Canada $132 million after a probe into the exchange of sensitive information about government bonds.

data.title: UnitedHealth Refutes WSJ Report on New DOJ Probe; Stock Tumbles Intraday
data.description:
UnitedHealth Group on Friday refuted claims by The Wall Street Journal that the US Department of Justice had launched an investigation into the health insurer's Medicare billing practices.

The Journal reported Friday, citing sources, that the DOJ was looking into UnitedHealth's protocol for recording diagnoses that prompt additional payments to its Medicare Advantage, or MA, plans. The Journal said that the new civil fraud investigation was launched in recent months.

Prior WSJ reports show that Medicare paid the company "billions of dollars" for dubious diagnoses, according to the latest report by the Journal. The Department of Health and Human Services' Office of Inspector General is also involved in the latest probe, the Journal reported Friday.

UnitedHealth's shares were down 8.9% in afternoon trade.

"We are not aware of the 'launch' of any 'new' activity as reported by the Journal," the company said in a statement. "Any suggestion that our practices are fraudulent is outrageous and false."

The government routinely reviews all MA plans to ensure compliance, UnitedHealth said.

"We can neither confirm nor deny the existence of any investigations, should there be any," a Department of Health and Human Services Office of Inspector General spokesperson told MT Newswires in an e-mailed statement. The DOJ didn't reply to a request for comment from MT Newswires.

Late last year, UnitedHealth and Amedisys agreed to extend the deadline of their proposed merger as the deal faced scrutiny from the DOJ over competition concerns.

data.title: Proto Labs Inc SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Proto Labs Inc, a leading digital manufacturing company specializing in custom parts production, has released its 2024 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the competitive custom parts manufacturing market.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenue: $500.9 million, a decrease of 0.6% compared to 2023, primarily due to a decline in Injection Molding and Sheet Metal revenue, partially offset by an increase in CNC Machining revenue.

Gross Profit: $223.2 million, an increase of 0.5% from 2023, with a gross margin of 44.6%, up from 44.1% in 2023, driven by focused management of resources and increased automation.

Income from Operations: $19.9 million, a decrease of 29.3% from 2023, impacted by $5.6 million in operating expenses associated with the closure of certain operations in Germany.

Net Income: $16.6 million, a slight decrease from $17.2 million in 2023, with a decrease in the provision for income taxes contributing to the net income.

Net Income Per Share: $0.66, consistent with 2023, reflecting stable earnings per share despite changes in revenue and operating income.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company reported revenue from four primary product lines: Injection Molding, CNC Machining, 3D Printing, and Sheet Metal. CNC Machining saw a 4.4% increase in revenue, while Injection Molding, 3D Printing, and Sheet Metal experienced declines of 4.8%, 0.6%, and 7.7% respectively.

Geographical Performance: Revenue in the United States decreased slightly by 0.2%, while Europe saw a 2.2% decrease. The company noted a positive impact of $1.9 million from foreign currency movements, primarily due to the strengthening of the British Pound and Euro against the U.S. Dollar.

Sales Units: The company served 51,552 unique customer contacts in 2024, a decrease of 3.6% from the previous year. Despite the decrease in customer contacts, revenue per customer contact increased by 3.1%.

New Production Launches: The company rebranded Hubs to the Protolabs Network by Hubs in January 2024, enhancing its global network of premium manufacturing partners and expanding its manufacturing capabilities.

Future Outlook: The company plans to continue investing in technology and manufacturing processes to meet the evolving needs of its customers. It also intends to expand its international sales efforts and explore opportunities in new geographic regions.

Operational Adjustments: The company announced the closure of its prototype injection molding manufacturing facility in Eschenlohe, Germany, and the discontinuation of Direct Metal Laser Sintering 3D printing services in Putzbrunn, Germany, with plans to complete these actions by fiscal year 2025.

Strategic Initiatives

Expansion of Digital Manufacturing Capabilities: Proto Labs has focused on expanding its digital manufacturing capabilities through strategic acquisitions and rebranding efforts. The acquisition of Hubs in 2021, rebranded as the Protolabs Network by Hubs in January 2024, has allowed the company to provide customers with access to a global network of premium manufacturing partners, complementing its in-house manufacturing capabilities. This initiative aims to increase the size, complexity, and breadth of manufacturing processes offered to customers, thereby enhancing the company's competitive position in the custom contract manufacturing market.

Capital Management: The company has actively managed its capital through share repurchase programs and investments in technology and infrastructure. In 2024, Proto Labs repurchased $60.3 million of its common stock, reflecting a strategic decision to return value to shareholders. Additionally, the company has invested in expanding its manufacturing capacity and enhancing its technology, with capital expenditures focused on property, equipment, and other capital assets. The company maintains a strong cash position, with $89.1 million in cash and cash equivalents as of December 31, 2024, and continues to generate positive cash flow from operations.

Future Outlook: Looking ahead, Proto Labs plans to continue investing in technology and manufacturing processes to meet the evolving needs of its customers. The company intends to expand its international sales efforts and explore opportunities in new geographic regions. Additionally, Proto Labs aims to enhance its e-commerce interface technology and internal software systems to support future growth. The company is committed to maintaining a strong balance sheet and believes its existing cash and cash equivalents, along with cash generated from operations, will be sufficient to meet its working capital and capital expenditure requirements for at least the next 12 months.

Challenges and Risks

Competitive Market: Proto Labs faces significant challenges in maintaining its competitive edge in the fragmented and highly competitive custom parts manufacturing market. The company must continuously innovate and expand its product lines to meet evolving customer needs and technological advancements. The closure of its manufacturing facilities in Germany and Japan highlights operational challenges in maintaining global operations.

Key Risks: The company identifies several key risks that could materially affect its business:

  • Competition: Proto Labs faces intense competition from a variety of custom parts manufacturers, brokers, and in-house production lines. Competitors may have greater resources and could adopt similar business models, potentially eroding Proto Labs' market share.
  • Technological Change: The company must keep pace with rapid technological advancements and develop new products and processes. Failure to do so could result in decreased demand for its offerings.
  • Operational Scaling: As the company grows, it must effectively scale its technology and processes to handle increased demand. Any failure in this area could harm customer retention and acquisition.
  • Cybersecurity: The storage of confidential customer information poses a risk of data breaches, which could damage the company's reputation and result in legal liabilities.
  • Global Economic Conditions: Economic uncertainty could impact customer demand and the company's ability to innovate. Global operations expose the company to risks such as currency fluctuations and compliance with diverse regulations.
  • Climate Change: Natural disasters and regulatory measures related to climate change could disrupt operations and increase costs.
  • Supply Chain: Dependence on single or limited source suppliers for critical materials poses a risk of supply disruptions.
  • Intellectual Property: The company may face challenges in protecting its intellectual property and could be subject to infringement claims.

Management Strategies: Management acknowledges the need to adapt to industry trends such as SKU proliferation, shorter product life cycles, and the shift to e-commerce sourcing. The company plans to invest in technology and expand its manufacturing capabilities to address these challenges. The closure of facilities in Germany and Japan is part of a strategic realignment to focus on more profitable operations.

Market Risks: Proto Labs is exposed to market risks, including fluctuations in foreign currency exchange rates, which could impact financial results. The company does not currently engage in hedging transactions to mitigate this risk, which could lead to volatility in reported revenue and expenses.

SEC Filing: Proto Labs Inc [ PRLB ] - 10-K - Feb. 21, 2025

data.title: NATURAL HEALTH TRENDS CORP SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Natural Health Trends Corp, a company specializing in direct-selling and e-commerce of wellness, herbal, beauty, lifestyle, home, daily care, and at-home test products, has released its annual Form 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the competitive market.

Financial Highlights

Net Sales: $42.963 million, a decrease of 2% from the previous year, primarily due to decreased sales in the Americas and Taiwan, offset by an increase in the Chinese e-commerce retail business.

Gross Profit: $31.785 million, representing 74.0% of net sales, slightly down from 74.6% in the previous year due to higher costs related to the Premium Noni juice product.

Loss from Operations: $(1.299) million, an improvement from $(1.671) million in the previous year, reflecting lower commissions and selling, general, and administrative expenses.

Net Income: $572 thousand, consistent with the previous year, despite a decrease in other income and a lower income tax provision.

Net Income Per Common Share: $0.05, unchanged from the previous year, with both basic and diluted shares outstanding remaining relatively stable.

Business Highlights

Geographical Performance: The company operates in multiple regions, with a significant presence in Greater China, which includes Hong Kong, Taiwan, and China. Hong Kong is a critical market, accounting for 82% of net sales, primarily from products delivered to members in China. The Americas, including the United States, Canada, and several Latin American countries, also contribute to the company's operations, though to a lesser extent.

Sales Units: As of December 31, 2024, the company had 30,870 active members, a decrease from 32,410 at the end of 2023. This decline reflects challenges in member retention and recruitment, impacting sales volumes.

New Product Launches: The company continuously sources unique and proprietary products, with a focus on immediate impact offerings. Product development is driven by market trends, new technologies, and scientific findings.

Future Outlook: The company plans to focus resources on promising markets, particularly Greater China, Southeast Asia, India, South America, and Europe. Efforts include establishing China-based manufacturing capabilities and increasing brand awareness. The company also aims to reapply for a direct selling license in China, which could enhance business operations if successful.

Operational Strategy: The company employs a network marketing system, leveraging personal recommendations to distribute products. This strategy is supported by a comprehensive training system and a multi-faceted promotional plan to engage and motivate members.

Regulatory Environment: The company operates under various regulatory frameworks, particularly in China, where direct selling and anti-pyramiding regulations are stringent. The company continues to adapt its business model to comply with these regulations and mitigate potential risks.

Member Support and Engagement: The company provides extensive support to its members through training, promotional events, and personalized services. This support is crucial for member retention and business growth.

Technology and Internet Initiatives: The company has invested in e-commerce capabilities, with most sales occurring online. This includes a global platform that allows members to sell products across all operational countries, enhancing the company's reach and efficiency.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: The company is focusing its resources on investing in its most important markets, particularly Greater China and regions where existing members can recruit prospects and sell products, such as Southeast Asia, India, South America, and Europe. The company is also investing in its Mainland China entity to establish China-based manufacturing capabilities, increase brand awareness, source more Chinese-made products, and build a chain of service stations.

Capital Management: The company declared and paid cash dividends of $0.20 per common share during each quarter of 2024 and 2023, totaling $9.2 million for each year. The Board of Directors authorized a stock repurchase program with $21.9 million remaining available for future purchases. The company maintains a strong liquidity position with $43.9 million in cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities as of December 31, 2024. The company also relocated its corporate headquarters from Hong Kong to California in February 2025.

Future Outlook: The company expects to continue paying a quarterly cash dividend of $0.20 per share for the foreseeable future. It plans to reapply for a direct selling license in China when circumstances are favorable, which could incrementally benefit its existing business. The company is also exploring opportunities to generate more funding from capital markets if necessary, although it currently believes its internal liquidity is adequate to fund normal operations and financial commitments.

Challenges and Risks

Geopolitical and Economic Risks: The political and economic landscape in Hong Kong and China is volatile, with increasing influence from the Chinese government and economic challenges that could adversely impact the company's operations and financial results.

Cash Flow and Financial Performance: The company has experienced negative operating cash flows in recent years, which could affect its ability to pay dividends, support operations, and maintain solvency.

Adverse Publicity: Negative media attention or public perception regarding the company's products or network marketing practices could harm its reputation and sales.

Regulatory Risks: Compliance with complex and evolving regulations in China and other markets is critical. Any violations or changes in laws could materially affect the company's business.

Product Concentration: The company relies on a limited number of products and suppliers, which poses a risk if demand decreases or supply is disrupted.

Competition: The direct-selling industry is highly competitive, with many larger and better-resourced competitors, which could impact the company's ability to recruit and retain members.

Member Network Risks: The company depends on its network of independent members for sales, and a decline in active members or their productivity could negatively impact revenue.

Cybersecurity and Data Privacy: The company is exposed to risks related to data breaches and compliance with privacy laws, particularly in China and Hong Kong.

Management’s Discussion and Analysis: Management acknowledges the challenges posed by geopolitical tensions, particularly between the United States and China, and the impact of tariffs and trade policies on global economic conditions. The company is focused on maintaining compliance with regulatory requirements and enhancing its product offerings to mitigate these risks.

Market Risk: The company is exposed to foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, especially given its significant revenue from international markets. The Hong Kong dollar's peg to the U.S. dollar provides some stability, but exposure to other currencies like the Chinese yuan and South Korean won could impact financial results. The company has not used foreign currency exchange contracts to mitigate these risks.

SEC Filing: NATURAL HEALTH TRENDS CORP [ NHTC ] - 10-K - Feb. 21, 2025

data.title: US Equity Indexes Drop as Worsening Consumer Sentiment, Output Growth Pushes Treasury Yields Lower
data.description:
US equity indexes declined in midday trading Friday following a drop in government bond yields after a consumer sentiment gauge was revised lower and private sector output growth unexpectedly slowed.

The Nasdaq Composite slumped 1.4% to 19,689.7, with the S&P 500 down 1.1% to 6,051.2 and the Dow Jones Industrial Average 1.3% lower at 43,603.9. All peer groups were in the red intraday, with consumer discretionary and industrials leading the decliners. The consumer staples sector was the sole gainer.

In economic news, the University of Michigan consumer sentiment index was revised downward on Friday to 64.7 for February from 67.8 in the preliminary estimate, compared with expectations for no revision in a survey compiled by Bloomberg. That latest print was below the upwardly revised final reading of 71.7 in January.

Respondents in the Michigan survey expected a 4.3% inflation rate over the next year and 3.5% annual inflation over the next five years, up from 3.3% and 3.2%, respectively, in January.

Output growth in the US private sector unexpectedly slowed in February as the services sector contracted, while optimism waned amid worries over the impact of tariffs and domestic spending cuts, S&P Global's (SPGI) flash purchasing managers' index showed. The composite output gauge fell to a 17-month low of 50.4 from 52.7 in January, compared with the 53.2 consensus in a survey compiled by Bloomberg.

The pace of annual rates for seasonally adjusted US existing home sales fell to 4.08 million in January from 4.29 million in December, compared with the 4.13 million expected in a survey compiled by Bloomberg, data from the National Association of Realtors showed. Sales were up 2% from a year earlier.

US Treasury yields fell, with the 10-year down 6.8 basis points to 4.43% and the two-year was 3.8 basis points lower at 4.23%.

Further, in economic news, the February flash reading of manufacturing conditions from S&P Global Friday rose to an eight-month high of 51.6 from 51.2 in January, above an expected 51.4 in a survey compiled by Bloomberg. The index indicates expansion, in line with the Empire State and the Philadelphia Fed manufacturing readings released earlier in the week.

In company news, UnitedHealth Group is under scrutiny by the US Department of Justice for allegedly encouraging doctors to record diagnoses that result in extra payments to the insurer's "Medicare Advantage" plans, the Wall Street Journal reported Friday, citing people familiar with the matter. Shares of the company slumped 8.8%, among the worst performers on the S&P 500.

Akamai Technologies has "limited" short-term growth opportunities, BofA Securities said in a note Friday, following the company's Q4 results overnight. BofA downgraded Akamai to neutral from buy and cut its price target to $100 from $125. Akamai shares sank 19.6% intraday, the steepest decliner on the S&P 500.

MercadoLibre's Q4 diluted earnings and sales late Thursday beat the average analyst estimate compiled by FactSet. Shares jumped 8.3% intraday, the top performer on the Nasdaq.

West Texas Intermediate crude oil futures slumped 2.1% to $70.97 a barrel.

Gold futures were fractionally lower at $2,955.4, and their silver counterparts dropped 1.2% to $33.4.

data.title: Manulife Financial Target Increased to $52, at BMO
data.description:
BMO has increased its one year target on Manulife Financial by $2, to $52, after its fourth-quarter beat.

Analyst Tom MacKinnon writes that fourth-quarter positives include strong Asia sales, better new business CSM growth, and better remittances ($7 billion, which, netting $0.75 billion reinsurance transaction capital free-up, equates to "impressive" 90% of 2024 core earnings).

Maintain Outperform.

data.title: Aluminum Pulls Back from 9-Month High
data.description:
Aluminum futures fell to $2,670 per tonne from the nine-month high of $2,730 on February 20th, as bets that the US government may relax some sanctions on Russia eased growing concerns of low supply.

The developments lifted Rusal HDRs, the world’s largest aluminum producer outside China, by 50% in the third week of February, reflecting expectations that the company may have access to new export partners.

This is despite fresh threats from the European Commission that it may place fresh sanctions on Russian aluminum.

Still, China produced 44 million tons of aluminum in 2024, the most on record, meaning that output will be forced to slow considerably this year as Beijing capped production at 25 million tons in 2017 to prevent excess supply and aid carbon emission targets.

This is despite the likely increase in the budget deficit to support fiscal stimulus, set to pick up winds of improving credit growth.

U.S. Offshore Rig Count 0 from Last Week to 14 — Baker Hughes
U.S. Gas Rig Count Down 2 from Last Week to 99 — Baker Hughes
FDA Approves Mirum Pharmaceuticals Treatment for Lipid-Storage Disease
data.description:
By Stephen Nakrosis

The Food and Drug Administration approved Mirum Pharmaceuticals' Ctexli to treat Cerebrotendinous Xanthoma, a very rare lipid-storage disease.

The approval makes Ctexli, or chenopod, the first FDA-approved drug to treat CTX, the agency said Friday.

Patients with CTX are unable to break down cholesterol in a normal way, which can result in damage to the brain, liver, and skin and tendons, the FDA said.

The FDA also said the recommended dosage is 250 milligrams, taken orally three times a day.

Write to Stephen Nakrosis at stephen.nakrosis@wsj.com
